meta.title: BSPN — Belarus Business Union: market entry, investments and government relations for foreign and local companies
meta.description: Since 1990 BSPN (Belarusian Union of Entrepreneurs and Employers named after Prof. M.S. Kunyavsky) defends business interests before Belarusian government authorities, helps foreign companies enter the Belarus market, supports inbound investment and provides legal expertise. ~150 member companies, 7 industry associations, 20+ government advisory councils.

onboarding.title: Solve what you can't move alone
onboarding.subtitle: BSPN unites 94+ active Belarusian companies. Since 1990 we bring business issues to government, drive legislative change and defend entrepreneurs in court. When the union speaks, the system listens.
onboarding.step1Desc: Describe what's blocking your business: inspections, rulings, contested norms. BSPN lawyers and experts take it on within 24 hours.
onboarding.step2Desc: Systemic issues are taken to inter-agency councils, working groups and meetings with ministries and Parliament.
onboarding.step3Desc: 35 years on: dozens of legislative amendments, overturned inspections and rulings, contracts worth millions defended.
onboarding.trust: 94+ companies from 14 industries already trust BSPN

about.pageDescription: The Belarusian Union of Entrepreneurs and Employers named after Prof. M.S. Kunyavsky — the country's leading business association since 1990
about.intro: BSPN brings together 94 organizations from 14 sectors. We represent business interests in 20+ collegial bodies under government agencies, providing legal protection, expert support and international business connections.
about.section1Desc: Social partnership, professionalism and voluntarism — the foundation of our work
about.section2Desc: BSPN's path since 1990 — over 35 years on the side of entrepreneurs
about.section3Desc: Board, Co-Chairs and the union's organizational structure
about.section4Desc: Concrete results: laws changed, rights protected, problems solved
about.section5Desc: 7 associations covering Belarus's key economic sectors

mission.quote: "Promoting entrepreneurship and protecting the lawful rights and interests of businesses is the foundation of Belarus's economic prosperity"
mission.quoteAuthor: — BSPN motto, based on the principles of Prof. M.S. Kunyavsky
mission.p1: BSPN is the largest business association in Belarus, founded in 1990. Our mission is to unite entrepreneurs to protect their interests, foster a healthy business climate and strengthen social partnership.
mission.p2: BSPN acts as an intermediary between business and government, taking part in 20+ collegial bodies under ministries and agencies of the Republic of Belarus. Our voice matters in the discussion of every key bill that affects business.
mission.value1Desc: We build constructive dialogue between business and government. BSPN is a bridge that ensures business interests are accounted for in economic policy.
mission.value2Desc: Our team — economists, lawyers and industry experts with deep experience. We provide qualified expertise on every aspect of running a business in Belarus.
mission.value3Desc: Membership in BSPN is a conscious choice by companies that understand the value of a collective business voice and shared problem-solving.
mission.value4Desc: We openly publish our results, hold regular reporting events and provide members with full access to information about union activities.
mission.symbolDesc: The BSPN symbol is "Veles" — the god of work and prosperity in Slavic mythology — embodying the creative force of entrepreneurship and wisdom in business.

history.pageDescription: From founding in 1990 to becoming Belarus's leading business association
history.memorialDesc: The union bears the name of Professor Mikhail Kunyavsky — an outstanding economist and one of BSPN's founders and first leader. His ideas of social partnership and constructive dialogue between business and the state remain the foundation of the union.
history.1990Desc: BSPN was established as one of the first business associations in the post-Soviet space.
history.1992Desc: First industry associations created, uniting companies by economic sectors.
history.1998Desc: BSPN joined international business organizations and built partnerships with European business associations.
history.2000Desc: The Directors Club was founded — a venue for discussing strategic questions of business development.
history.2005Desc: BSPN established an arbitration court for alternative dispute resolution.
history.2010Desc: The union's anniversary. By this time BSPN was represented in 15+ collegial state bodies.
history.2015Desc: New directions launched: data protection, digital transformation, startup support.
history.2020Desc: Adapting to new conditions: online consultations, remote events and digital member services.
history.2025Desc: Approved the 2026–2028 development strategy with a focus on digital transformation and international cooperation.
history.2026Desc: Launching the new website as a service hub and the strategy to attract new members.

leadership.leader1Desc: Coordinates the union's work with state bodies and international organizations.
leadership.leader2Desc: Oversees expertise, legal support and member relations.
leadership.structure1Desc: The supreme governing body. Held at least once every three years.
leadership.structure2Desc: Collegial executive body. Coordinates day-to-day activity.
leadership.structure3Desc: 7 staff members: economists, lawyers, international relations specialists.

achievements.pageDescription: Concrete BSPN results for Belarusian business
achievements.title: Key wins
achievements.ach1Desc: Initiated and pushed through Tax Code amendments that lowered the tax burden for small and medium business.
achievements.ach2Desc: Thanks to systematic work with the Ministry of Economy, requirements and timelines for business registration were reduced.
achievements.ach3Desc: Drafted and promoted recommendations limiting groundless inspections of businesses.
achievements.ach4Desc: Organized 10+ business missions to EU, CIS and Asian countries, generating 200+ business contacts.
achievements.ach5Desc: Launched the BSPN business ambassadors institute in 15+ countries to support exports and international cooperation.

associations.pageDescription: Seven sector associations under BSPN — uniting elevator, dental, tourism, woodworking, construction, non-food retail and SME-support businesses across Belarus
associations.intro: Each association is a sector platform within BSPN. We help companies from the same industry shape regulation together, exchange best practice and break into new markets. If your business operates in one of these sectors, a powerful union is already standing behind you.
associations.liftDesc: Manufacturers, installers and service operators of elevator equipment. Engages with Gospromnadzor and the Ministry of Housing & Utilities on technical regulations, certification and safe operation of elevators in residential and commercial buildings across Belarus.
associations.dentalDesc: Private dental clinics and practices. Defends interests before the Ministry of Health and during licensing; sets service standards, runs continuing education and group purchasing of consumables and equipment.
associations.nonfood: Association of Non-Food Producers and Retailers
associations.nonfoodDesc: Producers, importers and retail networks in non-food. Works with the Ministry of Antimonopoly Regulation and Trade (MART) on pricing, labelling, consumer extremism protection and competition policy.
associations.tourismDesc: Tour operators, travel agents, hotels and excursion services. Supports inbound tourism, digitalization, simpler visa rules and promotion of Belarus as a tourist destination.
associations.woodDesc: Timber processing, furniture, joinery and wooden construction materials. Works with the Ministry of Forestry and Bellesbumprom on export programs, FSC certification and access to raw materials.
associations.constructionDesc: Builders, design firms and construction-materials makers. Co-shapes Belarusian technical codes (TKP/TNPA) and state programs. Defends contractors in dealings with state customers and tender disputes.
associations.smesupportDesc: Business incubators, entrepreneurship centers, technology parks and SME finance funds. Coordinates Belarus's SME-support infrastructure and runs joint programs with the Ministry of Economy and the State Committee on Science & Technology.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: How BSPN processes and protects your personal data in accordance with the laws of the Republic of Belarus
privacy.intro: This Privacy Policy (the "Policy") describes how the Belarusian Union of Entrepreneurs and Employers named after Prof. M.S. Kunyavsky ("BSPN", "Operator", "we") processes and protects personal data of individuals who visit bspn.by or interact with us through any other channel. The Policy is drafted in accordance with the Law of the Republic of Belarus No. 99-Z of 7 May 2021 "On the Protection of Personal Data", the Law of the Republic of Belarus No. 455-Z of 10 November 2008 "On Information, Informatisation and Information Protection", and other applicable laws of the Republic of Belarus.

sections.title: 1. Personal Data Operator
paragraphs: The personal data operator is the Belarusian Union of Entrepreneurs and Employers named after Prof. M.S. Kunyavsky (short name — BSPN), payer's account number (UNP) 100073850, registered address: 22 Fabrichnaya Str., 220033, Minsk, Republic of Belarus.
Contact for the person responsible for the organisation of personal data processing: e-mail info@bspn.by, phone +375 17 210-18-42.

sections.title: 2. Scope
paragraphs: This Policy applies to all personal data BSPN receives from data subjects through bspn.by, membership applications, consultation requests, newsletter subscriptions, events, e-mail, telephone and other channels.
The Policy does not apply to third-party websites that may be linked from BSPN's site. BSPN bears no responsibility for the privacy practices of such third parties.

sections.title: 3. Categories of personal data
paragraphs: BSPN processes the following categories of personal data:
— full name, position, name of the organisation the data subject works for or represents;
— contact details: phone number, e-mail address, postal address;
— information voluntarily provided by the subject in forms, applications, requests, questionnaires;
— technical data: IP address, browser type and version, device type, operating system, referrer, pages visited, cookies and identifiers automatically transmitted when visiting the site;
— payment data for membership fees and services (processed via an acquiring bank — BSPN does not store card details).

sections.title: 4. Purposes and legal grounds
paragraphs: BSPN processes personal data exclusively for the following purposes:
— review of membership applications and conclusion of membership agreements;
— provision of consulting, legal, advocacy and information services to members and interested parties;
— organisation of participation in events, government councils, forums, working groups;
— delivery of information and promotional materials about BSPN's activities, subject to the data subject's consent;
— compliance with statutory obligations of BSPN under the laws of the Republic of Belarus (accounting, tax, statistical reporting);
— ensuring the security and proper functioning of the site, traffic analytics.

Legal grounds for processing are: the data subject's consent (Article 5 of Law No. 99-Z), performance of a contract to which the data subject is a party, compliance with statutory duties imposed on the Operator, and the legitimate interests of BSPN where they do not override the rights of the data subject.

sections.title: 5. Consent and its withdrawal
paragraphs: Consent to the processing of personal data is given by the data subject freely, specifically, knowingly and unambiguously — by ticking the appropriate box on the website form or by another act clearly indicating consent.

The data subject may withdraw consent at any time by sending a written request to info@bspn.by or to BSPN's postal address. BSPN will cease processing and erase the personal data within 15 working days of receiving the withdrawal, except where processing is required by the laws of the Republic of Belarus.

sections.title: 6. Transfer to third parties
paragraphs: BSPN does not sell personal data and does not transfer it to third parties for commercial purposes. Transfer is permitted only in the following cases:
— to authorised state authorities of the Republic of Belarus on the grounds and in the manner prescribed by law;
— to acquiring banks and payment operators — to the extent required for processing payments;
— to authorised employees of BSPN and engaged consultants acting under a contract that includes confidentiality obligations;
— to industry associations and partners of the Union — only with the data subject's explicit consent.
Any transfer is governed by an agreement that ensures an appropriate level of protection in accordance with Law No. 99-Z.

sections.title: 7. Cross-border transfers
paragraphs: Cross-border transfers of personal data are made only to countries that ensure an adequate level of protection for the rights of data subjects, or with the data subject's written consent in accordance with Article 9 of Law No. 99-Z.
The list of countries providing an adequate level of protection is approved by the National Personal Data Protection Center of the Republic of Belarus.

sections.title: 8. Retention periods
paragraphs: Personal data is retained for as long as necessary to achieve the purposes of processing, but no longer than:
— the term of the relevant agreement (membership, services) plus 3 years thereafter for dispute resolution;
— the period prescribed by the laws of the Republic of Belarus for accounting and tax records (typically not less than 5 years);
— until the consent is withdrawn — for data processed on the basis of consent (newsletters, marketing communications).

sections.title: 9. Security measures
paragraphs: BSPN takes all reasonable legal, organisational and technical measures to protect personal data from unlawful or accidental access, destruction, modification, blocking, copying, distribution or other unlawful actions:
— HTTPS/TLS encryption of data in transit;
— access controls for employees and confidentiality undertakings;
— regular backups and integrity monitoring;
— employee training on personal data protection;
— incident response logs and procedures.

sections.title: 10. Rights of the data subject
paragraphs: Pursuant to Articles 11–15 of Law No. 99-Z, the data subject has the right to:
— receive information about the processing of their personal data;
— modify, correct, erase or block their personal data;
— withdraw consent at any time;
— lodge a complaint with the National Personal Data Protection Center of the Republic of Belarus or with a court in the manner prescribed by law.
Requests are sent in writing to info@bspn.by or by post. Response time — within 15 working days.

sections.title: 11. Cookies and analytics
paragraphs: bspn.by uses cookies and similar technologies to ensure the functioning of the site, save user preferences (language, theme) and collect anonymous traffic statistics.
Third-party analytics services (where used) process data under their own policies. Users may disable cookies in their browser settings at any time, although this may limit website functionality.

sections.title: 12. Changes to the Policy
paragraphs: BSPN may amend this Policy. The current version is always available at bspn.by/legal/privacy. Material changes are additionally communicated to members by e-mail.

sections.title: 13. Supervisory authority
paragraphs: The supervisory authority for the protection of personal data in the Republic of Belarus is the National Personal Data Protection Center. Address: 14 Bersona Str., Minsk. Website: cpd.by.

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.subtitle: Conditions for using bspn.by, registering an Account and engaging with BSPN
terms.intro: These Terms of Use (the "Terms") govern the relationship between the Belarusian Union of Entrepreneurs and Employers named after Prof. M.S. Kunyavsky ("BSPN", "Union", "we") and any individual or legal entity ("User") that uses bspn.by and its services. The Terms are drafted in accordance with the Civil Code of the Republic of Belarus, the Law of the Republic of Belarus No. 90-Z of 9 January 2002 "On Consumer Protection", the Law of the Republic of Belarus No. 113-Z of 28 December 2009 "On Electronic Documents and Electronic Digital Signatures" and other applicable laws.

sections.title: 1. General provisions and acceptance
paragraphs: These Terms constitute a public offer within the meaning of Article 407 of the Civil Code of the Republic of Belarus. Use of the site, including Account registration, form submission or newsletter subscription, constitutes the User's full and unconditional acceptance of the Terms.
If the User disagrees with any part of the Terms, they must stop using the site.

sections.title: 2. Definitions
paragraphs: "Site" — the online resource located at bspn.by, including all subdomains and pages.
"User" — any person using the site in any form.
"Member" — a legal entity or sole entrepreneur that has entered into a membership agreement with BSPN.
"Account" — a private section of the site accessible to a registered User by login and password.
"Content" — text, graphic, audiovisual and other materials posted on the site.

sections.title: 3. Registration and Account
paragraphs: Registration is voluntary. Upon registration, the User must provide accurate data and keep it up to date.
The User is solely responsible for keeping their login and password confidential. All actions performed within the Account are deemed to have been performed by the User.
If credentials are compromised, the User must immediately notify BSPN at info@bspn.by.

sections.title: 4. Rules of use
paragraphs: When using the site, the User undertakes:
— to comply with the laws of the Republic of Belarus;
— not to infringe the rights and legitimate interests of BSPN or third parties;
— not to upload materials containing state, commercial or other legally protected secrets;
— not to engage in actions disrupting the normal functioning of the site (DDoS attacks, unauthorised access, distribution of malicious code).

sections.title: 5. BSPN services
paragraphs: BSPN provides members and other interested parties with information, legal, consulting, advocacy and educational services in accordance with the Union's bylaws and regulations.
The scope and procedure for the provision of services are determined by the membership agreement, supplementary agreements, and regulations of BSPN's councils and committees.
Information published openly on the site (news, analytics, explanations) is for reference only and does not constitute legal advice within the meaning of the laws on legal aid.

sections.title: 6. Membership fees and payment
paragraphs: Membership fee amounts and payment procedures are determined by BSPN's Presidium and recorded in the membership agreement.
Online payments are processed via an acquiring bank. BSPN does not store card details. Refunds are made in accordance with the Law on Consumer Protection and the agreement.

sections.title: 7. Intellectual property
paragraphs: All intellectual property on the site (texts, images, video, logos, design, source code) is owned by BSPN or used with the rights holders' permission and is protected under the copyright laws of the Republic of Belarus.
Use of the content without BSPN's written permission is not allowed, except for citation with attribution.

sections.title: 8. Prohibited actions
paragraphs: The User must not:
— share their credentials with third parties;
— use the site for spam, fraud or distribution of unlawful information;
— scrape or automatically collect data from the site without BSPN's written consent;
— reproduce, duplicate, copy or commercially exploit the site without BSPN's consent.

sections.title: 9. Liability
paragraphs: BSPN takes reasonable efforts to ensure that the site operates correctly but does not guarantee uninterrupted or error-free operation.
BSPN is not liable for any losses incurred by the User as a result of using or being unable to use the site, except as expressly required by the laws of the Republic of Belarus.
The User is responsible for the accuracy of the data provided and for all actions performed within their Account.

sections.title: 10. Force majeure
paragraphs: The parties are released from liability for partial or total failure to perform if it is caused by force majeure (natural disasters, military actions, changes in legislation, acts of public authorities, power or telecommunications outages).

sections.title: 11. Governing law and dispute resolution
paragraphs: The Terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of Belarus.

Disputes arising from use of the site and BSPN's services are resolved through negotiations. Failing agreement, disputes are referred to the BSPN Court of Arbitration (where an arbitration agreement is in place) or to the economic court at BSPN's seat under the Economic Procedure Code of the Republic of Belarus.

sections.title: 12. Changes to the Terms
paragraphs: BSPN may unilaterally amend the Terms. The current version is published at bspn.by/legal/terms and takes effect upon publication unless explicitly stated otherwise.
Continued use of the site after publication of the changes constitutes the User's agreement with the new version.

sections.title: 13. Details and contacts

Phone: +375 17 210-18-42, e-mail: info@bspn.by.

kunyavsky.pageTitle: Professor M.S. Kunyavsky — Founder of BSPN
kunyavsky.pageDescription: Mikhail Sergeyevich Kunyavsky — economist, scholar and the founding leader of the Belarusian Union of Entrepreneurs and Employers. His ideas of social partnership and constructive dialogue between business and state remain the foundation of BSPN.
kunyavsky.title: Professor M.S. Kunyavsky: scholar, citizen, founder
kunyavsky.intro: Belarus's largest business association — BSPN — bears the name of Professor Mikhail Sergeyevich Kunyavsky. It is a tribute to the man who, back in the early 1990s, understood that a market economy without a strong, organized business community would not survive — and took the steps to bring that community into being.
kunyavsky.section1Body: Doctor of Economic Sciences, professor. A long-standing leader and mentor whose academic work on enterprise management, economic restructuring and social partnership is still cited in Belarusian and international academia.
kunyavsky.section2Body: In 1990, under M.S. Kunyavsky's leadership, one of the first independent business associations in the post-Soviet space was established. Its mission — to represent the real sector in dialogue with the state, fight for predictable rules of the game and defend entrepreneurs through the difficult transition years.
kunyavsky.section3Body: Social partnership as a way of resolving conflicts among business, trade unions and government. Professionalism as a standard — economists and lawyers, not activists. Voluntary participation. Honest public dialogue. These principles are written into BSPN's statutes to this day.
kunyavsky.section4Body: In 2008 the BSPN Board established the union's highest award — the Crystal Vyales — awarded annually for contribution to the development of entrepreneurship in Belarus. Since 2007, when Mikhail Sergeyevich passed away, the union has officially carried his name. This is not a ceremonial gesture: it is a reminder that every decision of the union must have a human dimension and long-term meaning.
kunyavsky.fact4: Architect of social partnership in Belarus

symbolika.pageDescription: The story behind BSPN's emblem: Vyales, the ancient Slavic god of trade, craft and prosperity. The sign takes the form of a horn of plenty, while the wordmark «ВЯЛЕС» is set in old Slavic script from the era of Francysk Skaryna.
symbolika.title: Vyales — a sign carrying a thousand years of Belarusian entrepreneurship
symbolika.intro: When BSPN was founded in 1990, the founders wanted a symbol with no borrowings and no accidents — an image through which Belarusian business could speak about itself. That is how the Vyales sign was born.
symbolika.section1Body: In pre-Christian Belarusian mythology Vyales is the god of prosperity, trade and animal husbandry — the «cattle god», but cattle were the very measure of wealth in those days (hence «skotnitsa» as treasury, «skotolyubie» as love of money). Vyales's symbol is the horn of plenty: «from the horn comes plenty». Vyales's sacred river is the Viliya. Among the Krivichi and the Polotsk people he was more important than the warlike Perun: when merchant embassies reached Byzantium, they took oaths in the name of Vyales.
symbolika.section2Body: Belarusian tradition is foreign to militant mythology. A civilian, peaceful, creating god — Vyales — is the heir of the Krivichi and Polotsk merchants, the very first Belarusian entrepreneurs whose business activity built the economy of the region. BSPN is a continuation of that line.
symbolika.section3Body: The visual identity was designed by Aleg Surski — member of the Union of Artists of Belarus and today the President of the Belarusian Union of Designers. The sign is a stylized horn; the wordmark «ВЯЛЕС» is rendered in old Slavic script from the time of Francysk Skaryna. The result is an interlacing of eras: paganism, the craft tradition of the Polotsk school, Skaryna's printing legacy — and modern business.
symbolika.section4Body: The scholarly foundation behind the sign is research conducted by the Institute of History of the National Academy of Sciences of Belarus and historian Edward Mikhailovich Zaykovsky. We believe that a brand with genuine historical depth does not go out of style.
symbolika.section5Body: In June 2008 the BSPN Board established the union's highest award — the Crystal Vyales. Its composition repeats the emblem — the horn at the base, Slavic script above. The award is presented annually for outstanding contribution to the development of entrepreneurship in Belarus.
symbolika.altLogo: BSPN emblem — a stylized horn of plenty with the «Vyales» wordmark in old Slavic script

foreignBusiness.pageTitle: Belarus Market Entry for Foreign Companies — BSPN Advisory
foreignBusiness.pageDescription: BSPN helps foreign companies enter the Belarus market: company formation, banking, taxation, legal support, partner search, government relations and access to the EAEU through Belarus.

hero.title: Launch your business in Belarus — backed by a 35-year-old union that government listens to
hero.subtitle: BSPN — the Belarusian Union of Entrepreneurs and Employers named after Prof. M.S. Kunyavsky — accompanies foreign companies through every stage of entering the Belarus market: from company registration to representation in ministries. Entering Belarus means entering the EAEU.

foreignBusiness.whyTitle: Why a foreign business needs a local union
foreignBusiness.why1Body: 20+ ministerial advisory councils, direct working contacts and an understanding of unwritten rules. You save months.
foreignBusiness.why2Body: In inspections, licensing or tax disputes, BSPN stands on the member's side and files official requests on behalf of the union.
foreignBusiness.why3Body: Membership in BSPN signals to partners and banks that your company is part of Belarus's business community.
foreignBusiness.why4Body: Belarus is part of the Eurasian Economic Union. By incorporating here you gain tariff preferences across Russia, Kazakhstan, Armenia and Kyrgyzstan.
foreignBusiness.service1Body: Choosing the right legal form (LLC, representative office, joint venture), registration in Minsk and the regions, opening a bank account.
foreignBusiness.service2Body: Selecting the optimal tax regime. Assessing eligibility for HTP, the Great Stone industrial park, free economic zones, agro-eco-tourism preferences.
foreignBusiness.service3Title: Labor law, work permits and residence
foreignBusiness.service3Body: Hiring local talent, relocating foreign specialists, work permits, temporary and permanent residence.
foreignBusiness.service4Body: Access to the directory of 150+ BSPN member companies. Sourcing of suppliers, dealers, distributors and cooperation partners.
foreignBusiness.service5Body: Adapting policies to Belarus's Personal Data Law, OPD audits, registration with the National Personal Data Protection Center.
foreignBusiness.service6Body: BSPN Arbitration Court — fast and confidential resolution of commercial disputes. An alternative to state economic courts.
foreignBusiness.regime1Body: Profit-tax and social-contribution preferences for IT, software development and Web3 projects. BSPN guides residency and compliance.
foreignBusiness.regime2Body: Joint Belarus–China project. Up to 10 years of profit-tax exemption, customs and VAT relief for manufacturers.
foreignBusiness.regime3Body: Six FEZs — Minsk, Brest, Vitebsk, Gomel-Raton, Grodnoinvest, Mogilev. Tax preferences for export-oriented production.
foreignBusiness.regime4Body: Decree No. 6: profit-tax and local-fee preferences for production located outside Minsk and regional centers.
foreignBusiness.regime5Body: A special regime for tourism facilities in rural areas. A promising direction for foreign operators.
foreignBusiness.marketsTitle: Who benefits most from BSPN advisory
foreignBusiness.market1Body: HTP residency, hiring and retaining developers, services exports, IP protection.
foreignBusiness.market2Body: FEZ, Great Stone, production localization, supplier and contractor sourcing.
foreignBusiness.market3Body: Labelling, pricing, consumer extremism protection, access to retail networks.
foreignBusiness.market4Body: Belarus is a transit corridor between the EU and the EAEU. Customs, licensing and tariff support.
foreignBusiness.market5Body: Visa-free regime for 76 countries (30 days), support for hotel and resort investments.
foreignBusiness.market6Body: HTP is one of the most blockchain-friendly jurisdictions in the region.
foreignBusiness.ctaTitle: Ready to discuss your Belarus entry?
foreignBusiness.ctaBody: Send a request — we will connect you with a dedicated BSPN expert. The first consultation is free and confidential. We will help you decide whether you need a legal entity or a representative office is enough.

investments.pageTitle: Invest in Belarus — Investor Advisory by BSPN
investments.pageDescription: BSPN supports investment projects in Belarus: due diligence, jurisdiction selection, investment agreements with the state, investment protection, access to guarantees and preferences, M&A.

hero.title: Invest in Belarus — protected by a business union that ministries listen to
hero.subtitle: Since 1990 BSPN has represented investors before Belarusian government authorities. We help structure deals, conclude investment agreements with the state, navigate regulatory inspections and protect investments in disputes.

investments.why1Body: A single project in Belarus opens the way to a 180M+ market: Russia, Kazakhstan, Armenia, Kyrgyzstan. Tariff preferences and unified technical regulations.
investments.why2Body: Strong technical talent (especially in IT, engineering, chemistry, agri) at competitive labor cost.
investments.why3Body: Transit position between the EU, Russia and Ukraine. Developed rail network, freight capacity, warehousing and trade infrastructure.
investments.why4Body: HTP, Great Stone, six FEZs, small-city decrees — real tax incentives and simpler procedures.
investments.instr1Body: An agreement between an investor and the Republic of Belarus that grants tax and customs preferences, simplified land allocation and tailor-made conditions for major projects. BSPN advises on structure and accompanies negotiations.
investments.instr2Body: Belarus has BITs with 60+ countries that protect foreign investment from expropriation, guarantee repatriation of profit and provide access to international arbitration.
investments.instr3Body: Belarus's PPP Law applies to infrastructure, concessions, healthcare, transport and utilities. BSPN supports projects on the private partner side.
investments.instr4Body: Concession agreements for natural resources, infrastructure facilities and land. BSPN's specialised expertise covers approvals and tenders.
investments.instr5Body: Programs of the Development Bank, EDB and the Belarusian SME Finance Support Fund. BSPN helps select instruments and structure projects.
investments.regime1Body: 0% profit tax and VAT on HTP-resident services. Residents — IT, biotech, fintech, EdTech, Web3.
investments.regime2Body: Up to 10 years of profit-tax exemption, equipment-import benefits. Joint Belarus–China project.
investments.regime3Body: Six FEZs across the country. Preferences for export-oriented production.
investments.regime4Body: Tax preferences for production in small and mid-sized Belarusian cities.
investments.supportTitle: What BSPN does for an investor
investments.support1: Investment idea analysis and legal structuring
investments.support2: Optimal tax regime selection and investment-agreement negotiation
investments.support4: Protection of investor rights in inspections and disputes
investments.support5: Access to the directory of 150+ member companies for partners and sites
investments.support6: Representation in ministries and the Council on Entrepreneurship Development
investments.ctaBody: Send a brief description — we will come back with a preliminary assessment and an advisory proposal. Confidential.

partners.pageTitle: BSPN Partners — an ecosystem for Belarusian business
partners.pageDescription: Banks, audit firms, consultancies, business media, incubators and state development institutions — BSPN works with partners that bring real value to its members.
partners.intro: BSPN is more than a union of companies — it is an ecosystem of partners: financial institutions, professional services, state development bodies and business media. Partnership means discounts, joint programs and priority access for union members.
partners.groupFinanceDesc: Banks and funds supporting SME and investment projects
partners.groupMediaDesc: Information and analytical partners of the union
partners.p1Desc: Concessional finance, grants and SME training — a long-standing BSPN partner on support programs.
partners.p2Desc: Corporate banking, factoring, foreign-trade support for BSPN members.
partners.p3Desc: Management and finance consulting, expertise for business strategies.
partners.p4Desc: Legal support for cross-border projects, M&A and complex disputes.
partners.p5Desc: Belarus's leading business magazine for executives — official media partner of BSPN.
partners.p6Desc: Digital services for private healthcare — partner of the Dental Association.
partners.p7Desc: Online business publication: partnership content and joint research.
partners.p8Name: State Committee on Science and Technology
partners.p8Desc: Joint programs on innovative entrepreneurship and startup support.
partners.ctaDesc: If your company works with Belarusian business and is ready to bring value to our members — let's discuss the format of cooperation.

awards.pageTitle: BSPN Awards — Crystal Vyales and Notable Taler
awards.pageDescription: The highest awards of the Belarusian Union of Entrepreneurs and Employers: the Crystal Vyales for outstanding contribution to entrepreneurship in Belarus, and the Notable Taler — a distinction for business partners.
awards.intro: BSPN awards are public recognition of people and companies shaping the future of Belarusian business. Laureates are selected by the Board on the basis of yearly results and announced at BSPN's headline business events.
awards.velesDesc: BSPN's highest award. Granted for outstanding contribution to entrepreneurship, defence of business interests and strengthening the Belarusian economy. The trophy reproduces the union's emblem — the horn of plenty in the form of the stylized Vyales sign, executed in crystal and metal.
awards.velesCriteria: Criteria: a long business reputation, concrete results for the industry, an active stance within the business community.
awards.talerDesc: A distinction granted to partners and executives of BSPN member companies for supporting union projects, active engagement in associations and public councils, and contribution to social partnership.
awards.talerCriteria: Criteria: confirmed support of the union's initiatives, contribution to associations or the Directors Club, an impeccable business reputation.
awards.rule1: Nominations are submitted by the Board, the associations and BSPN members.
awards.rule2: Decisions are taken by the Board through a secret ballot.
awards.rule3: Awards are presented at the annual Congress or Business Forum.
awards.rule4: Laureates are published on the BSPN website and in Belarusian business media.

regulations.pageDescription: The principles by which the Business Union of Entrepreneurs and Employers operates: charter, Board regulations, decision-making procedures, transparency and accountability.
regulations.intro: A strong union is not only ideas — it is predictable procedure. BSPN's regulations describe how decisions are taken, how the union's position is formed and how members participate in governance.
regulations.block1Body: BSPN is a voluntary non-profit association of legal entities and individual entrepreneurs. It operates under the Charter approved by the Congress and the Civil Code of the Republic of Belarus. UNP 100073850.
regulations.block2Body: The supreme body is the BSPN Congress (held at least once every 3 years). Between congresses the union is led by the Board chaired by Co-Chairs. Day-to-day work is run by the Office and BSPN specialists.
regulations.block3Body: Decisions on union positions, legislative initiatives and major programs are taken by the Board through open vote. Strategic matters (Charter amendments, admission of new associations, reorganization) — only by the Congress with a qualified majority.
regulations.block4Body: Each year the Board publishes a union activity report — financial, legislative and representative. The report is available to all BSPN members and shared with business media and partners.
regulations.block5Body: Board members and Office staff follow the BSPN Code of Business Ethics. In case of a conflict of interest a Board member must declare it and abstain from voting.
regulations.downloadsHint: Documents are available in the member dashboard after sign-in.

specialists.pageTitle: BSPN Specialists — the team of experts
specialists.pageDescription: Lawyers, economists, sector coordinators and international cooperation specialists. The BSPN team is responsible for consultations, expertise on draft laws and member support.
specialists.intro: Behind every call to BSPN there is a specific specialist. A team of economists, lawyers and sector coordinators works directly with members — from first consultation to representation in government bodies.
specialists.areaLegalDesc: Corporate law, taxes, labor law, data protection, representation in courts and during inspections.
specialists.areaEconDesc: Bill analysis, sector reviews, regulatory impact assessment, preparation of union positions.
specialists.areaIntlDesc: Business missions, contacts with trade representations, support for exporters and investors.
specialists.areaAssocDesc: Curators of the 7 sector associations — a platform for participants of each industry.
specialists.areaEventsDesc: Congresses, Business Forum, Directors Club meetings, business breakfasts and thematic sessions.
specialists.areaCommsDesc: Media relations, member newsletters, digital products — website, dashboard, video blog.
specialists.ctaDesc: Email info@bspn.by or call +375 17 210-18-42 — we will connect you with the right specialist within the working day.

councils.pageTitle: Public Councils and BSPN Government Representation
councils.pageDescription: BSPN represents business interests in more than 20 collegial bodies attached to Belarusian ministries and agencies, as well as in BSPN's own Public Council on Economic Policy.
councils.intro: The voice of business must be heard where decisions are made. BSPN is a permanent participant in collegial bodies at ministries and in public councils where the regulation that governs entrepreneurship is shaped.
councils.groupOwnDesc: Union councils that shape industry positions
councils.groupGovDesc: 20+ collegial bodies at ministries and agencies
councils.ownEconBody: A standing council at BSPN. Analyses key economic initiatives of Belarus and prepares the union's public positions on tax, tariff, investment and monetary policy.
councils.govList: In particular — the Council for Entrepreneurship Development under the President of Belarus, public councils at the Ministry of Economy, Ministry of Taxes and Duties, MART, MNS, Ministry of Labor, Ministry of Industry, Ministry of Agriculture and Food, Ministry of Transport, Ministry of Housing and Utilities, Ministry of Justice, State Property Committee, Gosstandart, GKNT and others.
councils.value1: The chance to influence draft legislation before it is adopted
councils.value3: A direct channel to raise sector-level issues
councils.value4: Defending the interests of specific member companies in the right rooms

media.pageTitle: BSPN in the Media — publications, interviews, expert commentary
media.pageDescription: A curated set of BSPN appearances in Belarusian and international business media: interviews, analytics, legislative positions and expert commentary.
media.intro: BSPN is a permanent expert voice in Belarus's business media. Journalists turn to us for the business community's view on taxes, regulation, inspections, investment climate and international cooperation.
media.groupInterviewDesc: Conversations with BSPN leaders in major outlets
media.groupExpertDesc: BSPN's view on key market events
media.subscribeDesc: Subscribe to BSPN's weekly media digest — coverage in which the union and its members appear.

business.sectionDescription: Analytics, reviews and working materials — what BSPN prepares for its members and publishes for the market.

legislationReview.pageDescription: Weekly monitoring of changes in Belarus's tax, labor, corporate and regulatory legislation. BSPN positions on key draft laws.
legislationReview.intro: Understand the new rules before they come into force. The BSPN team of lawyers and economists tracks draft regulations, prepares concise briefings for members and forms a public union position on systemic changes.
legislationReview.block1Body: Changes to the Tax Code, rates, exemptions, special regimes (HTP, Great Stone, FEZ), MNS positions.
legislationReview.block2Body: Changes to the Civil Code and Economic Procedure Code, transactions, bankruptcy, corporate governance.
legislationReview.block3Body: Labor Code, occupational safety, flexible employment, specialist relocation, work permits.
legislationReview.block4Body: MART practice: pricing, labelling, advertising, consumer extremism protection.
legislationReview.block5Body: Personal Data Law and bylaws, the National Personal Data Protection Center, audits and approvals.
legislationReview.block6Body: EAEU customs regulation, currency control, services exports, foreign-trade licensing.
legislationReview.subscribeBody: A short digest in your inbox every Monday — what's new and what it means for business.

economy.pageTitle: Belarus Economy Review — the business community's view
economy.pageDescription: BSPN analytics on Belarus's key macroeconomic indicators: GDP, inflation, investment, exports. Business positions on tariff and monetary policy.
economy.intro: A monthly review by BSPN economists: what is happening to Belarus's economy, how it affects business, and what the union proposes to the regulator.
economy.block1Body: GDP, inflation, refinancing rate, FX — in plain language.
economy.block2Body: Capex dynamics, growth sectors, foreign direct investment.
economy.block3Body: Structure of exports, key markets, prospective directions for Belarusian exporters.
economy.block4Body: A report on the state of Belarus's business climate, constraints and union proposals.

socialLabor.pageDescription: BSPN's position on the National Council on Labor and Social Issues, the General Agreement and tripartite negotiations with the state and trade unions.
socialLabor.intro: BSPN is the employers' side in Belarus's social partnership system. We take part in shaping the General Agreement, sectoral tariff agreements and the work of the National Council on Labor and Social Issues.
socialLabor.block1Body: A tripartite agreement between Government, trade unions and employers. BSPN positions on pay, employment, occupational safety and social guarantees.
socialLabor.block2Body: Remote work, part-time, project-based formats. BSPN positions on Labor Code adaptation.
socialLabor.block3Body: Safety standards, accident prevention, member-only documents on compliance.
socialLabor.block4Body: A specific area BSPN runs jointly with trade unions and consumer organisations.

research.pageDescription: Applied research and member surveys by BSPN: business barriers, investment sentiment, regulatory assessment, business expectations.
research.intro: BSPN regularly surveys executives of member companies and publishes the results — so government decisions rest on real-world data and business has a public voice.
research.study1Body: A study on why Belarusian companies join business associations: legal clarification, interest representation, access to up-to-date information.
research.study2Body: Annual monitoring of constraints and drivers in Belarus's business climate.
research.study3Body: Regulatory, financial and talent barriers based on a survey of 100+ SME companies.

press.pageTitle: Business-Press Online — the BSPN business journal
press.pageDescription: BSPN's online journal for executives: bill briefings, member case studies, expert opinions and business events in Belarus.
press.intro: Business-Press is BSPN's own publication for executives. Here you find briefings and case studies — what is changing in the law, what experience members have gathered, what positions union experts take.
press.feature1Body: Concise briefings of the most important draft regulations with BSPN's stance.
press.feature2Body: Stories of how companies have solved typical Belarusian-business problems.
press.feature3Body: BSPN's lawyers, economists and sector coordinators on what hurts and what to change.
press.feature4Body: Announcements of congresses, forums, Directors Club meetings and BSPN thematic sessions.

experts.pageDescription: Practitioners who work systematically with government bodies, shape industry standards and help companies solve complex problems. Open access — union membership is not required.
experts.openTitle: You don't need to be a union member
experts.openBody: The expert catalog is open to laboratories, engineers, lawyers, journalists and regulators. No pre-baked scripts — just substantive conversation. If your question is systemic, we'll help raise it at the right level.

g1.name: Maxim S. Gorbatsevich
g1.since: 18+ years in the industry · Viena Medical since 2016
g1.bio: Founded and runs a company specialised in the pre-analytical phase of laboratory diagnostics. Previously — over a decade in the industry: service engineering, product portfolios, customers from district clinical labs to republican centres. Every principle the company lives by today grew out of that practice.
g1.philosophy: "The lab gets exactly what was specified — and shares responsibility for the result with us."
g1.role1: EFLM · expert consultant (European Federation of Clinical Chemistry and Laboratory Medicine; participant of the 2nd EFLM Strategic Conference, 2018)
g1.role2: ISO TC 212 · member of the international technical committee on clinical laboratory diagnostics, representative of the Republic of Belarus
g1.role3: Co-author of a scientific publication with Prof. Giuseppe Lippi (University of Verona) — scanning electron microscopy analysis of the inner diameter of 21G needles
g1.role4: Patent holder: a wearable scanner for laboratories with HL7 FHIR support (Belarus and international filing)
g1.role5: Speaker at EuroMedLab · WorldLab 2023, Rome — a platform for the "Uberisation" of lab services
g1.role6: Signatory of the Belarus–Vietnam intergovernmental agreement on healthcare cooperation (Minsk, 27 June 2017)
g1.topic2: Shift from procuring equipment & reagents to procuring lab tests
g1.topic3: Reforming MD registration: simplified entry for FDA/CE-cleared products
g1.topic4: Point-of-Care Testing (POCT) as a stand-alone regulatory category
g1.topic5: Allowing registered analogues onto "closed" laboratory systems
g1.topic6: Outsourcing of laboratory services and home-based medical care
g1.topic7: Standards ISO 6710, ISO 11137, ISO 13485, EU Directive 98/79/EC, CLSI, WHO
g1.topic8: Licensing of medical activity and innovative diagnostic services
g1.topic9: Antitrust oversight and reform of the public-procurement law
g1.topic10: Parallel imports and reform of medical-equipment servicing
g1.track: 8+ years of open dialogue with the Ministry of Health, MART, the State Control Committee and KGB. 11 systemic letters · 21 addressee agencies · 6 reform tracks — from a model technical specification for vacuum blood-collection systems (2016) to POCT regulation and licensing of innovative projects (2026).

experts.futureTitle: Are you an expert in your field?
experts.futureBody: If you're a practitioner who runs your own cases with government bodies, shapes standards or helps companies tackle systemic issues — tell us. BSPN helps experts reach the right councils, working groups and international committees.
